intro: Callicocca muscosa, also known as Wedelia or Callicocca, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to tropical America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, making it a valuable resource in folk medicine. The bioactive constituents of Callicocca muscosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Callicocca muscosa is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies for its purported health benefits.
